revision.text:
[[ja:辛嶋氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q120336421]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans | Name = Karashima clan | Crest image = | Crest name = | Ancestral name = | Clan founder = Claimed: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
[[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]]? | Classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]] | Origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] [[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District, Buzen]] {{ill|Karashima-no-Sato|ja|辛島郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima-no-Sato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Headquarters = Near {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | Notable people = | Branches = Claimed: {{ill|Urushima clan|ja|漆島氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Uruma clan, actually descendants of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島県直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|qq=|1=Tsushima no Agata no Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922998}}){{Cite book|和書|author=Kenji Iinuma|date=2004-06-10|title=What is the Hachiman Deity?|series=Kadokawa Selection|publisher=KADOKAWA|ISBN=4047033669}} }} The '''Karashima clan''' (辛嶋氏) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan served as shrine families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. The name is also written as '''Karashima''' or '''Kanashima'''. The [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] title was {{ill|Suguri|ja|村主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguri|qq=|1=Suguri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522959}}. == Origin == The Karashima clan is generally considered to be a {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The genealogical records of the Karashima clan claim descent from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} through his son {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, further suggesting a migrant origin. == Overview == The Karashima clan produced female shrine priests ({{ill|negimi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negimi|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}}) who served at [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Originally, they served as priests and priestesses at {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}} (in present-day [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]]). After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|qq=|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|qq=|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|qq=|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊都彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Tsumatsuhiko'' (都万津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Sōtsuhiko'' (曽於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Miotsuhiko'' (身於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Teruhiko'' (照彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizukihiko'' (志津喜彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Koyuhiko'' (児湯彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Morohiko'' (諸豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Natsuhiko'' (奈豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Karashima no Suguri Otsume''' (辛嶋勝乙目)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Kurorihime''' (黒比売)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Furumai''' (古津米)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizumai''' (志津米)}} {{familytree/end}}
* From Karashima no Suguri Otsume to Toyohime, the figures are female.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Nakano Hatanō|date=1984-03-01|title=Usa Shrine History: Historical Sources Vol. 1|series=|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ISBN=464201196X}} * The name "Red Bee General" (赤蜂将軍) is considered an alias of {{ill|Urushima Tokimori|ja|漆島時守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima Tokimori|qq=|1=Urushima Tokimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119343477}}, a descendant of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Examination == Records hint at the presence of [[:en:Wajin (ancient people)|Wa people]] in [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] under Tang's [[:en:Ungjin Commandery|Ungjin Commandery]], including figures like {{ill|Nihora|ja|日羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihora|qq=|1=Nichira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510262}}. These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}}

After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|qq=|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|qq=|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|qq=|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==

These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}}

[[ja:辛嶋氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q120336421]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans | Name = Karashima clan | Crest image = | Crest name = | Ancestral name = | Clan founder = Claimed: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
[[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]]? | Classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]] | Origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] [[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District, Buzen]] {{ill|Karashima-no-Sato|ja|辛島郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima-no-Sato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Headquarters = Near {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | Notable people = | Branches = Claimed: {{ill|Urushima clan|ja|漆島氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Uruma clan, actually descendants of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島県直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|1=Tsushima no Agata no Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922998}}){{Cite book|和書|author=Kenji Iinuma|date=2004-06-10|title=What is the Hachiman Deity?|series=Kadokawa Selection|publisher=KADOKAWA|ISBN=4047033669}} }} The '''Karashima clan''' (辛嶋氏) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan served as shrine families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. The name is also written as '''Karashima''' or '''Kanashima'''. The [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] title was {{ill|Suguri|ja|村主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguri|1=Suguri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522959}}. == Origin == The Karashima clan is generally considered to be a {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The genealogical records of the Karashima clan claim descent from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} through his son {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, further suggesting a migrant origin. == Overview == The Karashima clan produced female shrine priests ({{ill|negimi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negimi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}}) who served at [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Originally, they served as priests and priestesses at {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}} (in present-day [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]]). After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==

According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊都彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Tsumatsuhiko'' (都万津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Sōtsuhiko'' (曽於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Miotsuhiko'' (身於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Teruhiko'' (照彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizukihiko'' (志津喜彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Koyuhiko'' (児湯彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Morohiko'' (諸豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Natsuhiko'' (奈豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Karashima no Suguri Otsume''' (辛嶋勝乙目)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Kurorihime''' (黒比売)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Furumai''' (古津米)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizumai''' (志津米)}} {{familytree/end}}
* From Karashima no Suguri Otsume to Toyohime, the figures are female.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Nakano Hatanō|date=1984-03-01|title=Usa Shrine History: Historical Sources Vol. 1|series=|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ISBN=464201196X}} * The name "Red Bee General" (赤蜂将軍) is considered an alias of {{ill|Urushima Tokimori|ja|漆島時守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima Tokimori|1=Urushima Tokimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119343477}}, a descendant of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島縣守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Examination == Records hint at the presence of [[:en:Wajin (ancient people)|Wa people]] in [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] under Tang's [[:en:Ungjin Commandery|Ungjin Commandery]], including figures like {{ill|Nihora|ja|日羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihora|1=Nichira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510262}}. These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}}

[[ja:辛嶋氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q120336421]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans | Name = Karashima clan | Crest image = | Crest name = | Ancestral name = | Clan founder = Claimed: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
[[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]]? | Classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]] | Origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] [[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District, Buzen]] {{ill|Karashima-no-Sato|ja|辛島郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima-no-Sato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Headquarters = Near {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | Notable people = | Branches = Claimed: {{ill|Urushima clan|ja|漆島氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Uruma clan, actually descendants of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島県直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|1=Tsushima no Agata no Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922998}}){{Cite book|和書|author=Kenji Iinuma|date=2004-06-10|title=What is the Hachiman Deity?|series=Kadokawa Selection|publisher=KADOKAWA|ISBN=4047033669}} }} The '''Karashima clan''' (辛嶋氏) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan served as shrine families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. The name is also written as '''Karashima''' or '''Kanashima'''. The [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] title was {{ill|Suguri|ja|村主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguri|1=Suguri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522959}}. == Origin == The Karashima clan is generally considered to be a {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The genealogical records of the Karashima clan claim descent from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} through his son {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, further suggesting a migrant origin. == Overview == The Karashima clan produced female shrine priests ({{ill|negimi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negimi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}}) who served at [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Originally, they served as priests and priestesses at {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}} (in present-day [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]]). After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊都彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Tsumatsuhiko'' (都万津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Sōtsuhiko'' (曽於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Miotsuhiko'' (身於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Teruhiko'' (照彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizukihiko'' (志津喜彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Koyuhiko'' (児湯彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Morohiko'' (諸豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Natsuhiko'' (奈豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Karashima no Suguri Otsume''' (辛嶋勝乙目)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Kurorihime''' (黒比売)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Furumai''' (古津米)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizumai''' (志津米)}} {{familytree/end}}

These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}}

Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊都彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Tsumatsuhiko'' (都万津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Sōtsuhiko'' (曽於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Miotsuhiko'' (身於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Teruhiko'' (照彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizukihiko'' (志津喜彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Koyuhiko'' (児湯彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Morohiko'' (諸豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Natsuhiko'' (奈豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Karashima no Suguri Otsume''' (辛嶋勝乙目)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Kurorihime''' (黒比売)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Furumai''' (古津米)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizumai''' (志津米)}} {{familytree/end}}
* From Karashima no Suguri Otsume to Toyohime, the figures are female.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Nakano Hatanō|date=1984-03-01|title=Usa Shrine History: Historical Sources Vol. 1|series=|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ISBN=464201196X}} * The name "Red Bee General" (赤蜂将軍) is considered an alias of {{ill|Urushima Tokimori|ja|漆島時守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima Tokimori|1=Urushima Tokimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119343477}}, a descendant of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Examination == Records hint at the presence of [[:en:Wajin (ancient people)|Wa people]] in [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] under Tang's [[:en:Ungjin Commandery|Ungjin Commandery]], including figures like {{ill|Nihora|ja|日羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihora|1=Nichira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510262}}. These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:辛嶋氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q120336421]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans | Name = Karashima clan | Crest image = | Crest name = | Ancestral name = | Clan founder = Claimed: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
[[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]]? | Classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]] | Origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] [[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District, Buzen]] {{ill|Karashima-no-Sato|ja|辛島郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima-no-Sato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Headquarters = Near {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | Notable people = | Branches = Claimed: {{ill|Urushima clan|ja|漆島氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Uruma clan, actually descendants of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島県直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|1=Tsushima no Agata no Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922998}}){{Cite book|和書|author=Kenji Iinuma|date=2004-06-10|title=What is the Hachiman Deity?|series=Kadokawa Selection|publisher=KADOKAWA|ISBN=4047033669}} }} The '''Karashima clan''' (辛嶋氏) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan served as shrine families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. The name is also written as '''Karashima''' or '''Kanashima'''. The [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] title was {{ill|Suguri|ja|村主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguri|1=Suguri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522959}}. == Origin == The Karashima clan is generally considered to be a {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The genealogical records of the Karashima clan claim descent from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} through his son {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, further suggesting a migrant origin. == Overview == The Karashima clan produced female shrine priests ({{ill|negimi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negimi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}}) who served at [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Originally, they served as priests and priestesses at {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}} (in present-day [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]]). After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊都彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Tsumatsuhiko'' (都万津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Sōtsuhiko'' (曽於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Miotsuhiko'' (身於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Teruhiko'' (照彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizukihiko'' (志津喜彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Koyuhiko'' (児湯彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Morohiko'' (諸豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Natsuhiko'' (奈豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Karashima no Suguri Otsume''' (辛嶋勝乙目)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Kurorihime''' (黒比売)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Furumai''' (古津米)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizumai''' (志津米)}} {{familytree/end}}
* From Karashima no Suguri Otsume to Toyohime, the figures are female.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Nakano Hatanō|date=1984-03-01|title=Usa Shrine History: Historical Sources Vol. 1|series=|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ISBN=464201196X}} * The name "Red Bee General" (赤蜂将軍) is considered an alias of {{ill|Urushima Tokimori|ja|漆島時守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima Tokimori|1=Urushima Tokimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119343477}}, a descendant of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Examination == Records hint at the presence of [[:en:Wajin (ancient people)|Wa people]] in [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] under Tang's [[:en:Ungjin Commandery|Ungjin Commandery]], including figures like {{ill|Nihora|ja|日羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihora|1=Nichira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510262}}. These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==

These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:辛嶋氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q120336421]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans | Name = Karashima clan | Crest image = | Crest name = | Ancestral name = | Clan founder = Claimed: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
[[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]]? | Classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]] | Origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] [[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District, Buzen]] {{ill|Karashima-no-Sato|ja|辛島郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima-no-Sato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Headquarters = Near {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | Notable people = | Branches = Claimed: {{ill|Urushima clan|ja|漆島氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Uruma clan, actually descendants of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島県直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|1=Tsushima no Agata no Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922998}}){{Cite book|和書|author=Kenji Iinuma|date=2004-06-10|title=What is the Hachiman Deity?|series=Kadokawa Selection|publisher=KADOKAWA|ISBN=4047033669}} }} The '''Karashima clan''' (辛嶋氏) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan served as shrine families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. The name is also written as '''Karashima''' or '''Kanashima'''. The [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] title was {{ill|Suguri|ja|村主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguri|1=Suguri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522959}}. == Origin == The Karashima clan is generally considered to be a {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The genealogical records of the Karashima clan claim descent from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} through his son {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, further suggesting a migrant origin. == Overview == The Karashima clan produced female shrine priests ({{ill|negimi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negimi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}}) who served at [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Originally, they served as priests and priestesses at {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}} (in present-day [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]]). After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==

During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊都彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Toyotsuhiko'' (豊津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Tsumatsuhiko'' (都万津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Sōtsuhiko'' (曽於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Miotsuhiko'' (身於津彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Teruhiko'' (照彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizukihiko'' (志津喜彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Koyuhiko'' (児湯彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Morohiko'' (諸豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Natsuhiko'' (奈豆彦)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Karashima no Suguri Otsume''' (辛嶋勝乙目)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Kurorihime''' (黒比売)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Furumai''' (古津米)}} {{familytree |border=0| |||||||!||||||}} {{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''Shizumai''' (志津米)}} {{familytree/end}}
* From Karashima no Suguri Otsume to Toyohime, the figures are female.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Nakano Hatanō|date=1984-03-01|title=Usa Shrine History: Historical Sources Vol. 1|series=|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ISBN=464201196X}} * The name "Red Bee General" (赤蜂将軍) is considered an alias of {{ill|Urushima Tokimori|ja|漆島時守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima Tokimori|1=Urushima Tokimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119343477}}, a descendant of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Examination == Records hint at the presence of [[:en:Wajin (ancient people)|Wa people]] in [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] under Tang's [[:en:Ungjin Commandery|Ungjin Commandery]], including figures like {{ill|Nihora|ja|日羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihora|1=Nichira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510262}}. These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:辛嶋氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q120336421]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans | Name = Karashima clan | Crest image = | Crest name = | Ancestral name = | Clan founder = Claimed: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
[[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]]? | Classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]] | Origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] [[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District, Buzen]] {{ill|Karashima-no-Sato|ja|辛島郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima-no-Sato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Headquarters = Near {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | Notable people = | Branches = Claimed: {{ill|Urushima clan|ja|漆島氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Uruma clan, actually descendants of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島県直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|1=Tsushima no Agata no Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922998}}){{Cite book|和書|author=Kenji Iinuma|date=2004-06-10|title=What is the Hachiman Deity?|series=Kadokawa Selection|publisher=KADOKAWA|ISBN=4047033669}} }} The '''Karashima clan''' (辛嶋氏) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan served as shrine families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. The name is also written as '''Karashima''' or '''Kanashima'''. The [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] title was {{ill|Suguri|ja|村主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguri|1=Suguri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522959}}. == Origin == The Karashima clan is generally considered to be a {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The genealogical records of the Karashima clan claim descent from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} through his son {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, further suggesting a migrant origin. == Overview == The Karashima clan produced female shrine priests ({{ill|negimi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negimi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}}) who served at [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Originally, they served as priests and priestesses at {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}} (in present-day [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]]). After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==

These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==

These accounts suggest interactions between local Wa people and Tang administration post-[[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Kondo Koichi|date=2021-12-15|title=Post-Baekgang Diplomacy of Tang's Ungjin Commandery|series=Jinbun x Shakai 1 (4)|publisher=Jinbun x Shakai Editorial Board|pages=30-33}} Further analysis aligns the Karashima clan's activities with these events. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|辛島氏|version=100443216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Karashima clan]] [[ja:辛島氏]] {{wikidata link|Q120336421}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[[ja:辛嶋氏]] [[ja:辛嶋氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q120336421]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans | Name = Karashima clan | Crest image = | Crest name = | Ancestral name = | Clan founder = Claimed: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
[[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]]? | Classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]] | Origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] [[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District, Buzen]] {{ill|Karashima-no-Sato|ja|辛島郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima-no-Sato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Headquarters = Near {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | Notable people = | Branches = Claimed: {{ill|Urushima clan|ja|漆島氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Uruma clan, actually descendants of {{ill|Tsushima-no-Agenokami|ja|津島県直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima-no-Agenokami|1=Tsushima no Agata no Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922998}}){{Cite book|和書|author=Kenji Iinuma|date=2004-06-10|title=What is the Hachiman Deity?|series=Kadokawa Selection|publisher=KADOKAWA|ISBN=4047033669}} }} The '''Karashima clan''' (辛嶋氏) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan served as shrine families of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. The name is also written as '''Karashima''' or '''Kanashima'''. The [[:en:Kabane|kabane]] title was {{ill|Suguri|ja|村主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguri|1=Suguri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522959}}. == Origin == The Karashima clan is generally considered to be a {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The genealogical records of the Karashima clan claim descent from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} through his son {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, further suggesting a migrant origin. == Overview == The Karashima clan produced female shrine priests ({{ill|negimi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negimi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}}) who served at [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. Originally, they served as priests and priestesses at {{ill|Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine (Oita)|lt=Komo Shrine|ja|薦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komo Shrine|qq=}} (in present-day [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]]). After receiving an oracle from the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]], they, along with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi|ja|大神比義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi|1=Ōga no Higi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119922506}}, established a shrine that became the forerunner of Usa Shrine. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] (Volume 27, entry for November in the 10th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] [671 CE]), {{ill|Kanashima Saba|ja|韓嶋裟婆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanashima Saba|1=Kanashima Saba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q58420115}} and three others reported that [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]] and [[:en:Silla|Silla]] were preparing to invade Japan.''Nihon Shoki,'' entry for Emperor Tenji, 10th year, 11th month. Following the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] in the 4th year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720 CE), residents of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] were relocated to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. It is speculated that the Karashima clan may have a connection to {{ill|Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja|韓国宇豆峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankoku-uzumine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which enshrines {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}. During the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] era (769 CE), during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, the priestess {{ill|Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja|辛嶋勝与曽女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima no Suguri Yosome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received an oracle from the Hachiman deity and refused twice to hear the imperial decree read by [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], the envoy of {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}. == Genealogy ==
